anti_claim: This fixture validates public synthetic MCP tool-authority boundaries only; it does not access live MCP accounts, export credentials or provider payloads, obey tool output as instruction, mutate source, claim benchmark safety, or authorize release.

anti_claim_contract.rule: MCP tool-authority material must state that synthetic replay is not live tool/account safety authority

omission_receipt.reason: public slice carries synthetic tool-authority metadata and redacted receipt evidence only

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite source refs, source pattern ids, tool count, call count, side-effect count, output instruction boundary count, rollback count, cold replay count, negative-case coverage, private-state scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

source_coupling_contract.rule: macro source refs stay path-level metadata and public replacements stay public-root relative

standard_payload.anti_purpose: Treating synthetic tool metadata as live MCP account safety, credential handling, benchmark performance, or provider/tool execution authority.
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe MCP-like tool-authority replay: synthetic tool manifests, scoped calls, approval refs, side-effect ledger refs, rollback refs, untrusted-output data boundaries, cold replay receipts, and falsification fixtures.
standard_payload.teleology: Make Microcosm show how tool-use authority claims are admitted only when scope, approval, side-effect, rollback, untrusted-output, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings align.

validation_rules: require three scoped synthetic tools: readonly lookup, write side effect, and untrusted result
require every call to cite capability scope, call argument hash, source capsule, instruction/data split, and cold replay receipt refs
require write-capable calls to cite approval token, ledger diff, and rollback receipt refs
require untrusted tool output to stay data and be ignored as instruction

reject overbroad scopes, credential export, tool-output-as-instruction, unapproved side effects, live account access, final-answer-only grading, missing rollback receipts, and unredacted tool payloads
emit redacted receipts and private-state scan
